blocked_items.title: WealthAI Targets Fragmented Tech Stack Problem With AI-Native OS for Wealth Managers
Flanks Deploys AI Financial Advisor Capability Directly into Wealth Manager Workflows
AI Poised to Reshape Wealth Management Workflows and Client Engagement by 2026
Nitrogen Embeds Agentic AI Engine Into Advisor Workflow, Automating Pre/During/Post Meeting Tasks
Avaloq Positions AI and Digital Infrastructure as Non-Negotiable for Thailand's Wealth Firms
Altruist's AI Tax Planning Tool Triggers 6–9% Stock Drop at Schwab, LPL and Raymond James
blocked_items.reason: Fabrication detected: Quoted phrase 'what-if' scenarios: source uses 'what-if' — PASS, but the summary attributes the phrase to Altruist's press release; source confirms this.; Quoted phrase 'within minutes': source states Altruist said tax plans produced 'within minutes' — PASS.; Quoted phrase 'expands what a single advisor can handle': source attributes this to Jason Wenk — PASS.; Quoted phrase 'makes average advice a lot harder to justify': source attributes this to Jason Wenk — PASS.; Number in 'the so what': '1,600 RIA firms signed in the first month post-launch' — this number does NOT appear in the source text and is NOT supported anywhere in the source. The source only mentions Lifeworks Advisors ($900M RIA) as a single named signing. This specific figure (1,600 RIA firms) is fabricated and not found in the source, with no truncation explanation since the source covers the topic directly.; Number in 'the so what': '5,500 advisors' — PASS, appears in source.; Key stat '6–9%': source states 'fell between roughly 6% and 9%' — this is near-verbatim support for the 6–9% figure — PASS.; The 'so what' claims '1,600 RIA firms signed in the first month post-launch' — the source does not mention this figure anywhere and directly discusses Altruist's scale without referencing 1,600 RIA firms, making this a fabricated specific statistic contradicting the source's omission in a directly relevant passage.
